quizQuestion:
El médico le recomendó que {___} más.
(The doctor recommended that he rest more.)
sentenceTranslation: The doctor recommended that he rest more.
explanation: descansara — imperfect subjunctive of descansar (él/ella). Medical advice often uses recomendar/aconsejar que + imperfect subjunctive. Pattern: descansara, descansaras, descansara...

quizQuestion:
Era mejor que {___} menos café.
(It was better that we drink less coffee.)
sentenceTranslation: It was better that we drink less coffee.
explanation: bebiéramos — imperfect subjunctive of beber (nosotros). -er nosotros form has a written accent: bebiéramos. After era mejor que (impersonal past trigger), use imperfect subjunctive.

quizQuestion:
Si {___} más cuidadoso, no habrías tenido el accidente.
(If you were more careful, you wouldn't have had the accident.)
sentenceTranslation: If you were more careful, you wouldn't have had the accident.
explanation: fueras — imperfect subjunctive of ser (tú). Mixed conditional: si + imperfect subj (past counterfactual fueras = if you had been) + conditional perfect (no habrías tenido). Ser/ir tú form: fueras.

quizQuestion:
Si {___} más tiempo, haríamos más.
(If we had more time, we would do more.)
sentenceTranslation: If we had more time, we would do more.
explanation: tuviéramos — imperfect subjunctive of tener (nosotros). Note the written accent: tuviéramos. Type 2 si-clause: si tuviéramos (nosotros form) + conditional haríamos.

quizQuestion:
Si {___} más ejercicio, te sentirías mejor.
(If you exercised more, you would feel better.)
sentenceTranslation: If you exercised more, you would feel better.
explanation: hicieras — imperfect subjunctive of hacer (tú). Type 2 si-clause with hacer. Si hicieras más ejercicio = if you exercised more. This is a common type 2 pattern with lifestyle advice.

quizQuestion:
Ojalá {___} quedarnos más tiempo aquí.
(I wish we could stay here longer.)
sentenceTranslation: I wish we could stay here longer.
explanation: pudiéramos — imperfect subjunctive of poder (nosotros). Ojalá + imperfect subjunctive expresses an unlikely wish. Nosotros form: pudiéramos (note accent).

quizQuestion:
Espero que {___} bien a vuestra destino.
(I hope you (pl.) have arrived safely at your destination.)
sentenceTranslation: I hope you (pl.) have arrived safely at your destination.
explanation: hayáis llegado — present perfect subjunctive, vosotros form. Hayáis = vosotros form of haber in present subjunctive. Used with vosotros (Spain only). After espero que + completed action.

quizQuestion:
Si {___} ayuda, no habría fracasado.
(If he had asked for help, he would not have failed.)
sentenceTranslation: If he had asked for help, he would not have failed.
grammarPointLabel: pluperfect subjunctive: hubiera + pedido — si-clause type 3
explanation: hubiera pedido — pluperfect subjunctive, yo/él form. Type 3 si-clause: si hubiera pedido (he didn't ask, counterfactual) + no habría fracasado (conditional perfect). Pedido = past participle of pedir.

quizQuestion:
Complete the type 2 si-clause: '{___}'
Meaning: If he wanted to, he could do it.
sentenceTranslation: If he wanted to, he could do it.
explanation: Si quisiera, podría hacerlo. Quisiera = imperfect subjunctive of querer. Podría = conditional of poder. Type 2: hypothetical present/future condition.

correctAnswer: Si hubieras esperado, habrías podido venir.
acceptableAlternatives: Si hubieses esperado, habrías podido venir.
quizQuestion:
Complete the type 3 si-clause: '{___}'
Meaning: If you had waited, you would have been able to come.
sentenceTranslation: If you had waited, you would have been able to come.
grammarPointLabel: si-clause type 3: si hubieras esperado... habrías podido
explanation: Si hubieras esperado, habrías podido venir. Type 3: si + pluperfect subj (hubieras esperado — you didn't wait) + conditional perfect (habrías podido — you would have been able to).
targetLanguageWord: Si hubieras esperado, habrías podido venir.

quizQuestion:
Para el viernes, {___} la respuesta.
(By Friday, you will have received the answer.)
sentenceTranslation: By Friday, you will have received the answer.
explanation: habrás recibido — future perfect, tú form. Para el viernes = by Friday (deadline trigger). Habrás = tú form of haber in future. Recibido = past participle of recibir.

quizQuestion:
{___} antes, pero no sabía tu número.
(I would have called earlier, but I didn't know your number.)
sentenceTranslation: I would have called earlier, but I didn't know your number.
grammarPointLabel: conditional perfect: habría + llamado (yo) — standalone with pero
explanation: habría llamado — conditional perfect. Used without an explicit si-clause when the reason/condition is explained with pero (but). This shows a past action that didn't happen due to circumstances.

quizQuestion:
El ladrón {___} por la policía.
(The thief was arrested by the police.)
sentenceTranslation: The thief was arrested by the police.
grammarPointLabel: ser + past participle passive: fue detenido (masc.) + por-agent
explanation: fue detenido — ser passive (preterite). El ladrón is masculine: detenido. Agent: la policía introduced by por. Detenido = past participle of detener.

quizQuestion:
{___} trabajadores cualificados para el proyecto.
(Qualified workers are needed for the project.)
sentenceTranslation: Qualified workers are needed for the project.
explanation: Se necesitan — se-passive with plural subject. Trabajadores (pl.) → necesitan (pl.). This is how job listings and notices express impersonal need in Spanish.

quizQuestion:
Los libros {___} en la estantería por tema.
(The books are arranged on the shelf by topic.)
sentenceTranslation: The books are arranged on the shelf by topic.
grammarPointLabel: estar + past participle: están ordenados (masc. pl.)
explanation: están ordenados — estar + past participle (resultative state). Los libros is masculine plural: ordenados (agreeing in gender and number). This describes the current arrangement as a state.

quizQuestion:
Me contó que {___} a alguien muy interesante.
(Direct speech: 'He conocido a alguien muy interesante.' He told me he had met someone very interesting.)
sentenceTranslation: He told me he had met someone very interesting.
grammarPointLabel: reported speech backshift: present perfect → pluperfect (he conocido → había conocido)
explanation: había conocido — in reported speech, present perfect (he conocido) shifts to pluperfect (había conocido). This is the same backshift as from the preterite: both shift to pluperfect in reported speech.

quizQuestion:
{___} sea difícil, lo intentaré.
(Even if it's difficult, I'll try.)
[Hint: unknown outcome — subjunctive follows]
sentenceTranslation: Even if it's difficult, I'll try.
grammarPointLabel: concessive connector: aunque + subjunctive (even if — hypothetical)
explanation: aunque — with subjunctive (sea) = 'even if it's difficult' (we don't know yet). With indicative: aunque es difícil = 'even though it is difficult' (we know it's a fact). This aunque + subj/indic distinction is a core B2 skill.

quizQuestion:
{___} lo sabes, cuéntamelo todo.
(Since you know it, tell me everything.)
sentenceTranslation: Since you know it, tell me everything.
explanation: ya que — 'since / seeing that'. Followed by indicative (lo sabes = you know it = a fact). Ya que is used when the cause is considered obvious or already known to the listener.

quizQuestion:
No asistió a la reunión, {___} perdió información clave.
(He didn't attend the meeting, consequently he missed key information.)
sentenceTranslation: He didn't attend the meeting, consequently he missed key information.
explanation: por consiguiente — 'consequently / therefore'. Formal consecutive connector, more formal than así que. Common in academic writing and formal speech. Synonym: en consecuencia.

quizQuestion:
No salgas {___} yo llegue.
(Don't leave until I arrive.)
[Hint: future event — subjunctive follows]
sentenceTranslation: Don't leave until I arrive.
grammarPointLabel: temporal connector: hasta que + subjunctive (until — future event)
explanation: hasta que — 'until'. With subjunctive when referring to a future event: hasta que yo llegue = until I arrive (I haven't arrived yet). With indicative for past facts: esperé hasta que llegó = I waited until he arrived.

quizQuestion:
Saldré {___} me lo impidan.
(I'll leave unless they prevent me.)
sentenceTranslation: I'll leave unless they prevent me.
grammarPointLabel: conditional connector: a menos que + subjunctive (unless)
explanation: a menos que — 'unless'. Always followed by subjunctive: a menos que me lo impidan (unless they prevent me). This is a negative condition: the main action will happen UNLESS the subordinate condition is met.

quizQuestion:
Llegará {___} las cinco de la tarde.
(He'll arrive around five in the afternoon.)
sentenceTranslation: He'll arrive around five in the afternoon.
explanation: por — expressing approximate time. Por las cinco = around five o'clock. Contrast with para: para las cinco (by five o'clock = deadline). Por has the sense of 'somewhere around / approximately'.
